## Commerce Secretary Lutnick Urges Samsung and SK Hynix to Expand US Chip Output

US government pressure to onshore memory manufacturing could accelerate capex obligations and reshape the cost structure for Samsung and SK Hynix; for holders of either name, this is a policy tail risk that could compress margins if subsidies do not fully offset incremental build costs.

- US Commerce Secretary Lutnick publicly called on both Samsung and SK Hynix to grow domestic chip manufacturing capacity.
- The push reflects the ongoing US onshoring agenda and US–China semiconductor policy tensions.
- Both leading Korean memory suppliers are named; no specific investment commitments cited in the headline.
- Geopolitical pressure introduces regulatory uncertainty into Korean chipmakers' capital allocation decisions.
